2eaad.content:
## Commands

- Use `uv run zhar set-status <id> <status>` to move nodes through valid lifecycle states.
- Use `uv run zhar remove <id>` only for incorrect or intentionally transient records.
- Use `uv run zhar prune ...` with filters and preferably `--dry-run` first when reconciling many unexpected nodes.

## Development Context

When developing zhar inside this repository, prefer `uv run zhar` so the CLI reflects the current workspace source tree rather than an older installed entrypoint.

## Constraint

Prefer status transitions over deletion whenever the node should remain part of project history.

353ca.summary: Relation expansion is currently limited to component_rel adjacency and depends on shared component identity for broader traversal
353ca.content:
## Context

Relation-depth expansion now supports both the legacy architecture-local relation model and an optional generic cross-group link model.

## Current rule

Namespace-scoped traversal for show and export starts from the seed set and keeps the active status and tag boundary hard.

## Mechanisms

- `architecture_context/component_rel` still expands through shared `from_component` / `to_component` endpoints.
- An optional custom `links` group can add generic node-to-node edges, typically with `links/node_link` records that carry `metadata.from_id` and `metadata.to_id`.

## Optional-group behavior

If the `links` group is not loaded, relation-depth silently ignores that mechanism. If a saved link edge points at a node that is no longer available, read paths skip the dangling edge instead of failing.

## Write-time integrity

When the `links` group is loaded, link writes stay strict: new link nodes must declare existing endpoint IDs.

## Export behavior

Default exports omit the optional `links` group itself, but relation-depth can still use its edges to bring linked nodes from other loaded groups into the rendered result under their actual group headers.

## Implication

Broader relation webs now belong to normal memory traversal semantics without requiring a base-node links field or a second storage subsystem.

38680.summary: Memory mutation workflow supports literal bodies, stdin/env input, and attached supplemental notes
38680.content:
## Scope

This workflow covers durable memory mutations performed through the CLI rather than file-level implementation details.

## Mutation inputs

- Use `--content TEXT` when you already have the literal markdown body in the command invocation.
- Use `--content -` to read the body from stdin.
- Use `--from-env NAME` or `--content-var NAME` only when `NAME` is an environment variable that already contains the full body text.

## Shell rule

This surface reads environment variables, not shell-local variables. In PowerShell, set `$env:NAME = @'...'@` before invoking zhar. Passing `$NAME` to `--from-env` is wrong unless that value is itself the name of an existing environment variable.

## Non-memory-backed add behavior

For non-memory-backed node types, `zhar add ... --from-env NAME` and `zhar add ... --content-var NAME` create an attached `notes/note` record automatically instead of inline node content.

## Metadata rule

When a metadata field is backed by a `Literal` constraint, operator-facing guidance should enumerate the accepted values. For `decision_trail/research_finding`, the allowed `outcome` values are `adopted`, `rejected`, or `deferred`.

## Boundary

This workflow is semantically about how operators add and enrich memory records, so it belongs in architecture_context as operational context, with code_history only as a supplemental breadcrumb.

393df.summary: Memory maintenance workflow uses show/query/export confirmation plus scan, verify, and gc at the right choke points
393df.content:
## Confirmation

After a mutation, confirm resulting state with show, query, or export depending on whether you need node-level, filtered, or snapshot-level visibility.

## Validation

Use scan after marker edits, verify after structural changes, and gc at commit or cleanup choke points.

## Intent

These are operational semantics for maintaining trustworthy memory state, not just file-level implementation details.

## Boundary

This workflow belongs in architecture_context as technical operating context and should exist independently of any specific code_history file breadcrumb.

3b6dc.summary: Transient session runtime tracks show/export inspection state in temp session files
3b6dc.content:
## Summary

zhar now keeps transient CLI session state outside durable memory under the OS temp directory. The session runtime records shallow and expanded show interactions, surfaces session state in export runtime context, and gates challenge escalation through effective facts such as session_challenge_enabled and session_challenge_agent.

415c5.summary: Session runtime temp cache moved from .zhar/session to zhar_cache/session
415c5.content:
Transient session files now live under the OS temp directory at `zhar_cache/session` instead of `.zhar/session`.

This avoids collisions with project-root discovery and clarifies that the runtime session store is disposable cache state rather than project memory.

45939.summary: Relation-depth now supports optional links-group edges in addition to component_rel adjacency
45939.content:
## Context

Relation-depth expansion now supports both the legacy architecture-local relation model and an optional generic cross-group link model.

## Current rule

Namespace-scoped traversal for show and export starts from the seed set and keeps the active status and tag boundary hard.

## Mechanisms

- `architecture_context/component_rel` remains a descriptive architecture node type that explains relationships between components.
- `component_rel` neighborhood expansion still works through shared `from_component` / `to_component` endpoints, but that is separate from generic node linking.
- An optional custom `links` group can add generic node-to-node edges, typically with `links/node_link` records that carry `metadata.from_id` and `metadata.to_id`.

## Optional-group behavior

If the `links` group is not loaded, relation-depth silently ignores that mechanism. If a saved link edge points at a node that is no longer available, read paths skip the dangling edge instead of failing.

## Write-time integrity

When the `links` group is loaded, link writes stay strict: new link nodes must declare existing endpoint IDs.

## Export behavior

Default exports omit the optional `links` group itself, but relation-depth can still use its edges to bring linked nodes from other loaded groups into the rendered result under their actual group headers.

## Implication

Broader relation webs now belong to normal memory traversal semantics without requiring a base-node links field or a second storage subsystem.

465f4.summary: Relation-depth traversal now uses built-in links edges
465f4.content: Relation-depth traversal for zhar show and zhar export now uses the built-in links/node_link group. component_rel remains a descriptive architecture node type rather than a traversal mechanism. The links group is always available without repo-local initialization, traversal keeps the active status and tag boundary hard, dangling links are ignored on read, and writes still require existing endpoints. Default exports omit the links group unless it is requested explicitly.
